title: AMD Q1 2026 Earnings: Revenue Surges 37.8% on AI Infrastructure Growth
text: AMD delivered impressive first-quarter results, with revenue reaching $10.25 billion, significantly outpacing analyst expectations of $9.90 billion. This represented a robust 37.8% year-over-year surge, driven primarily by heightened demand for AI infrastructure. The semiconductor company's adjusted earnings per share hit $1.37, exceeding the consensus estimate of $1.29. Adjusted EBITDA climbed to $2.75 billion, surpassing the projected $2.20 billion. Operating margins improved to 14.4%, compared to 10.8% in the year-ago period. Data center operations emerged as the growth engine, with revenue expanding 57% year-over-year. CEO Lisa Su noted that 'the data center has become the primary engine of revenue and earnings growth,' fueled by increasing adoption of EPYC processors and Instinct GPUs among both cloud and enterprise customers expanding their AI capabilities. For Q2 2026, AMD projected revenue of $11.2 billion at the midpoint, above market expectations of $10.53 billion. Management expressed confidence in meeting growth targets through 2027 despite ongoing supply chain tightness. The company's performance underscores the accelerating investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure across the industry, positioning AMD competitively in this high-growth market segment.
